text:
Once upon a time in a small medieval village, there lived an old owl named Oliver.

Oliver loved nothing more than perching on towering oaks and listening to the quiet songs of nocturnal creatures. One crisp autumn evening, as he fluffed his feathers against the chill, he noticed something unusual.

A tiny figure had fallen out of an old oak tree branch—a squirrel whose summer journey had exhausted him. Oliver swooped down calmly. Usually fierce, he felt something else tonight.

He grabbed the squirrel gently with his sharp claws.

"What's this?" Oliver whispered.

The squirrel chirped. Life just wasn't hard enough.

Just then, a flock of doves flew overhead, mourning the forest's lost leaf.

Oliver placed the tiny creature back onto its perch.

"You'll find your way," Oliver soothed.

For days, Oliver took solitary walks. He would wander further than the village ever went, discovering wildflowers unknown and ancient woodlands.
